input.background: A manufacturer of industrial wheels suspects that profitable orders are being lost due to the long time taken to develop price quotes for potential customers. To investigate, 50 requests for price quotes were randomly selected from the set of all quotes made last year, and the processing time was determined for each quote. Each quote was classified as 'lost' or 'not lost' based on whether the customer placed an order after receiving a price quote.
input.data:
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}

input.question: Use a statistical software package to create a stem-and-leaf display for these data. Then shade each leaf of the display that corresponds to a lost order. Interpret the result.

output.answer: We used Minitab to generate the stem-and-leaf display in Figure 2.13. Note that the stem (the second column of the printout) consists of the number of whole days (digits to the left of the decimal). The leaf (the third column of the printout) is the tenths digit (first digit after the decimal) of each measurement. Thus, the leaf 2 in the stem 20 (the last row of the printout) represents the time of 20.23 days. Like the histogram, the stem-and-leaf display shows the shaded 'lost' orders in the upper tail of the distribution.
